Orchestrator fix-forward in round 2 introduced new helper scripts/resolve-close-report.sh and replaced the inline block with a 1-line invocation.

files_modified_in_round2_fix_forward: commands/commit.md (Step 3 — 1-line script invocation replaces 16-line inline path-walk)
files_new_in_round2_fix_forward: scripts/resolve-close-report.sh (new helper, 32 lines incl. 14 lines of leading comment)
scope.tagging_mechanism: Mechanism (ii) — inspector-side tagging with introduced_in_diff per AC-2.6 default-safe rule
scope.default_safe_contract: Findings with introduced_in_diff:true are NEW-in-this-diff. Findings with introduced_in_diff:false are pre-existing/advisory/non-blocking. Round 2: 'introduced_in_diff' covers the SAME cycle's combined fix-forward diff (round-1 + round-2 deltas both count as in-diff).

context.task_lineage: Round 2 re-close inspection. Round 1 had 5 findings (1 pre-existing, 4 NEW minor) on the 3 hooks files. Round 2 adds the round-2 fix-forward delta (new helper script + commit.md Step 3 1-line replacement) and surfaces 3 NEW findings from that delta plus 1 numeric correction to a carried-forward finding.
context.prior_blocker_status_round1: RESOLVED — `_resolve_context` dead function DELETED from hooks/posttool-subagent-track.py. AST FunctionDef walk confirms no `_resolve_context` exists. Only an audit comment at L440-442 remains (intentional historical breadcrumb, codex-confirmed acceptable).
context.prior_close_no_reason_round1: RESOLVED — round-1 CLOSE: NO was triggered by /commit Standard 1: a 16-line inline bash path-walk in commands/commit.md Step 3 violated the orchestrator-prompt-purity / no-inline-helper principle. Round-2 fix-forward extracted that block into scripts/resolve-close-report.sh and replaced it with a 1-line invocation. Verified in this report's verifications.round2_fix_forward_validation.commit_md_step3_change.
context.diff_state: Working-tree diff vs HEAD: commands/commit.md (modified Step 3, +5/-2 lines net), scripts/resolve-close-report.sh (untracked NEW file, 32 lines), scripts/INDEX.md + scripts/README.md auto-bumped with the new entry. The 3 hooks files are unchanged from round 1 (already committed in 28a1e85).

naming_violations.reason: Symbol named with leading underscore (private convention) but imported cross-module from hooks/posttool-allowlist-consume.py at lines 110, 115, 130, 132. Either it is a public API (drop the underscore) or callers should use a public wrapper.
naming_violations.recommendation: OBSERVATION_ONLY — naming-convention question affects pre-existing API surface (posttool-allowlist-consume.py import predates this cycle's diff scope). Rename would touch out-of-scope code beyond the 3-file diff cleanliness gate. Surfaced from codex consultation; logged for separate-cycle consideration.
naming_violations.rationale_non_blocking: Default-safe rule for ambiguous diff-introduction (file-level finding) plus scope-expansion concern: fixing requires editing hooks/posttool-allowlist-consume.py which is outside the 3 files in scope.

non_functional_files.reason: File mode is 644 (NOT executable). Every peer scripts/*.sh in the same directory is mode 755. The script has a `#!/bin/bash` shebang but cannot be invoked as `./scripts/resolve-close-report.sh`. The script functionally works at its only call site (commands/commit.md:54 invokes via `bash <path>` which does not require the execute bit), but the convention drift is real and confusing for future direct-execution callers.
non_functional_files.recommendation: chmod 755 scripts/resolve-close-report.sh. Codex confirmed PROPOSED_FIX (round 2 codex item 1).
non_functional_files.rationale_non_blocking: Functionally works via `bash <path>` invocation; no runtime failure. Convention-consistency drift only.

historical_docs.reason: Module docstring for `match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command()` says 'returns True iff ...' but the function signature is `dict | None` (returns the matched entry, not bool). Also misrepresents `args_contain` semantics as 'structured-equality on op/target/args' when args_contain is correctly documented (in the function's own docstring at L438-455) as a substring-on-arg-tail predicate.
historical_docs.recommendation: Reword to: 'returns the matched allowed_operations[] entry iff the parsed command structurally satisfies one entry; op/target match by equality, and optional args_contain[] fragments are checked only against the parsed argument tail.'
historical_docs.rationale_non_blocking: Documentation accuracy issue in module docstring; does not affect functional correctness or hook behavior. Cosmetic minor.

historical_docs.reason: TestReadGrant class docstring at L119 says 'public API, exact_or_substr semantics' but the read_grant function was changed in this same diff (cycle Item D / AC-D1) from exact_or_substr to exact_only. The class also contains two NEW tests added by this diff (test_read_grant_exact_only_rejects_substring at L161, test_read_grant_exact_only_rejects_write_against_todowrite at L180) that explicitly verify exact_only behavior — making the class docstring contradict the tests it now contains.
historical_docs.recommendation: Change docstring to: 'Unit tests for read_grant (public API, exact_only literal semantics).'
historical_docs.rationale_non_blocking: Documentation accuracy issue inside a test file's class docstring; does not affect functional correctness, hook behavior, or test discovery. Cosmetic minor.

historical_docs.reason: TestSentinelGrantLifecycle class docstring at L371-377 says 'four mandatory terminal-consumption cases' but the class enumerates five distinct case names by string (success, failure, non_zero, malformed, comment_only) AND contains EIGHT test_* methods (AST-verified in round 2: test_terminal_consume_success, test_terminal_consume_failure, test_terminal_consume_non_zero, test_terminal_consume_malformed, test_comment_only_attack_pretool_denied_no_leftover, test_terminal_consume_round_trip_unlinks_grant, test_sentinel_predicate_never_substring_matches_command_line, test_expired_grant_treated_as_missing). NOTE (round-2 correction): Round-1 report incorrectly stated '7 tests' — codex caught this in round 2; AST count is 8.
historical_docs.recommendation: Rewrite the class docstring to describe terminal consumption plus predicate/expiry coverage without a numeric count. Suggested wording: 'Covers terminal-consumption results (success, failure/non_zero, malformed, comment_only) plus round-trip matching, no-raw-substring matching, and expiry reap.' Codex confirmed PROPOSED_FIX (round 2 codex item 6).
historical_docs.rationale_non_blocking: Documentation accuracy issue inside a test file's class docstring; does not affect functional correctness, hook behavior, or test discovery.

historical_docs.reason: Comment-vs-code drift: leading comment at L2-8 advertises 'subproject path-walk' as the resolution strategy, but the implementation at L20-23 is a hard-coded 3-candidate probe (${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}/docs/dev/..., /root/.claude/docs/dev/..., ${CONTROL_ROOT}/docs/dev/...) — there is no actual path-walk (no dirname, no walk-upward, no commonpath derivation). When CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR is unset, the first candidate collapses to a literal '/docs/dev/close-report-${TASK_ID}.md' (FS-root-anchored) — a non-empty but bogus path that '[ -n $candidate ]' does not reject (the empty-prefix guard at L24 only catches the empty-string case, not the FS-root degenerate case). The '[ -f $candidate ]' check at L24 still works correctly (the bogus file does not exist) so there is NO runtime defect — the loop continues to the next candidate and the script's outcome is unchanged. Still, comment-vs-code mismatch is real cleanliness drift.
historical_docs.recommendation: Two acceptable fixes: (a) reword the leading comment to drop 'subproject path-walk' phrasing and describe the actual 3-candidate ordered probe; or (b) guard the project candidate with '[ -n ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-} ]' before adding it to the candidate list. Codex flagged this as the only substantive NEW concern in round 2 (codex item 3).
historical_docs.rationale_non_blocking: No runtime failure — '[ -f ]' correctly rejects the FS-root-anchored bogus path, loop continues to next candidate, final fallback at L31 still emits the correct CONTROL_ROOT path for error messages. The behavior matches the intent; only the comment overpromises. Cosmetic documentation drift, not a functional defect.

imports.reason: `from datetime import datetime, timezone` imports `timezone` but the local scope (inside `load_sentinel_grant_for_task` at L378) never references `timezone` — only `datetime.fromisoformat()` is used. The companion site at line 542 in reap_expired_sentinel_grants correctly imports only `datetime`. The L419 import was introduced when the sentinel-grant lifecycle was added in this cycle.
imports.recommendation: Change `from datetime import datetime, timezone` to `from datetime import datetime`. Codex re-confirmed PROPOSED_FIX (round 2 codex item 5).
imports.rationale_non_blocking: Unused import in a narrow local scope; no runtime impact, no namespace pollution at module level. Cosmetic minor.

shell_conventions.reason: Script uses `set -u` only — no `-e` (errexit), no `-o pipefail`. Every one of the ~40 peer scripts/*.sh files uses at minimum `set -e`, or more commonly `set -uo pipefail` / `set -euo pipefail` (grep ^set scripts/*.sh confirms: 33 use `set -euo pipefail`, 4 use `set -uo pipefail`, 4 use `set -e`, 1 outlier is this new script). Given the script has explicit `exit 0` / `exit 1` lines and contains no pipelines, missing flags do not produce a runtime defect — but they break the established peer-script convention, which is a real cleanliness signal for future maintainers.
shell_conventions.recommendation: Change L16 from `set -u` to `set -euo pipefail` to match the most-common peer convention. Codex confirmed PROPOSED_FIX (round 2 codex item 2).
shell_conventions.rationale_non_blocking: No runtime failure mode given explicit exit codes and no pipelines. Convention-consistency drift only.

_resolve_context_removed.method: AST FunctionDef walk + grep across hooks/ (re-verified in round 2)
_resolve_context_removed.result: PASS — no FunctionDef named _resolve_context exists in hooks/posttool-subagent-track.py. Two textual references remain: (a) audit comment at L440-442 (intentional historical breadcrumb, codex-confirmed acceptable per round 2 item 8), (b) backward-doc reference inside _resolve_base_context docstring at L417 (explicitly worded 'replaces the previous `_resolve_context` gate-and-bail pattern' — not stale, explains the supersession).
_resolve_context_removed.audit_comment_assessment: ACCEPTABLE — intentional historical breadcrumb. Codex re-confirmed in round 2: 'acceptable historical breadcrumb, not stale cruft.'
_resolve_context_removed.backward_reference_assessment: ACCEPTABLE — the docstring at L417 explicitly uses the word 'previous' and explains what replaced the removed helper. Deliberate cross-reference, not stale documentation.

round2_fix_forward_validation.method: git diff inspection + ls -la mode check + grep ^set on peer scripts + AST walk on tests
round2_fix_forward_validation.commit_md_step3_change: PASS — 16-line inline `CONTROL_ROOT=/root\nCLOSE_REPORT=...` block replaced by 1-line `CLOSE_REPORT="$(bash ~/.claude/scripts/resolve-close-report.sh "$TASK_ID")" || true`. The `|| true` is intentional: the helper exits 1 when no candidate exists while still emitting the fallback path on stdout for the downstream file-exists check. Codex confirmed commands/commit.md:54 is acceptable (round 2 codex item 4 OBSERVATION_ONLY).
round2_fix_forward_validation.new_helper_registration: PASS — scripts/resolve-close-report.sh is registered in scripts/INDEX.md:71 (auto-generated entry), scripts/README.md:52 (auto-generated entry), and referenced by commands/commit.md:49 (documentation) and commands/commit.md:54 (invocation). Codex confirmed no missing registration (round 2 codex item 9).
round2_fix_forward_validation.new_helper_mode_drift: FINDING (round-2 NEW) — mode 644, peer convention 755. Logged under non_functional_files[].
round2_fix_forward_validation.new_helper_set_flag_drift: FINDING (round-2 NEW) — `set -u` only; peer convention `set -euo pipefail` (or similar). Logged under shell_conventions[].
round2_fix_forward_validation.new_helper_comment_vs_code_drift: FINDING (round-2 NEW) — comment claims 'subproject path-walk' but implementation is hard-coded 3-candidate probe. Logged under historical_docs[].
round2_fix_forward_validation.test_count_correction: ACCEPTED (round-2 correction) — round-1 report said TestSentinelGrantLifecycle has 7 tests; round-2 AST walk verified 8 tests. Updated finding wording in historical_docs[] for L371-377.

function_reference_audit.method: AST FunctionDef enumeration + grep across hooks/, commands/, agents/, scripts/
function_reference_audit.allowlist_py: 14 top-level symbols (1 NamedTuple class, 4 private helpers, 8 public functions, 1 module constant). All referenced — public functions across 2-9 external files; private helpers via internal calls within the same file or cross-module imports.
function_reference_audit.posttool_subagent_track_py: 27 top-level functions after H restructure. All referenced internally by _main or its callees. No dead helpers remain.
function_reference_audit.test_allowlist_consolidation_py: All TestCase classes/methods auto-discovered by unittest. Referenced by hooks/tests/_final_sweep.sh and project index files.

no_other_cleanliness_violations.naming_conventions_overall: consistent — sentinel-grant lifecycle uses verb_object_for_context() pattern; tests use TestX class + test_x method convention; new bash script uses kebab-case filename matching peer convention. One naming-vs-visibility friction noted in naming_violations[] (pre-existing).
no_other_cleanliness_violations.duplicate_content: none — all 5 files have distinct purposes (lib helper, runtime hook, unit tests, command spec, new helper script)

verdict_input_for_close.explanation: Prior CLOSE: NO trigger (round 1 Bullet 1: /commit Standard 1 violation — 16-line inline path-walk in commit.md Step 3) is RESOLVED by the round-2 fix-forward: the inline block has been replaced by a 1-line invocation of new helper scripts/resolve-close-report.sh. The 8 remaining cleanliness findings (5 carried from round 1 + 3 NEW from round-2 fix-forward) are all minor advisory items: (1) file mode 644 drift, (2) `set -u`-only flag drift, (3) comment-vs-code drift in helper script (no functional bug, just doc-vs-code mismatch), (4) unused timezone import, (5) module docstring stale wording, (6) TestReadGrant docstring stale wording, (7) TestSentinelGrantLifecycle docstring stale-count wording, (8) pre-existing private-name cross-module import (observation_only). NONE has blocks_close:true. Per Section 5.4 rule 3 and AC-2.6 Mechanism (ii) tagging, file-level minor findings are non-blocking when verdict_input_for_close.close_blocking_findings=0. Cleanliness gate (round 2): PASS.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Round 2 codex challenge surfaced 3 NEW findings from the round-2 fix-forward delta (file mode 644 drift, set-u-only flag drift, comment-vs-code drift in the new helper script) plus 1 numeric correction to a carried-forward finding (TestSentinelGrantLifecycle has 8 tests, not 7 as round-1 report stated). Carried-forward doc/import/test-docstring drift items were re-confirmed minor with their proposed fixes unchanged. Codex final assessment: 'I see no close-blocker. The only substantive extra concern is item 3 [comment-vs-code drift in the new helper]; otherwise findings are cosmetic/minor.' OBSERVATION_ONLY items: commands/commit.md:54 1-line invocation is acceptable (|| true intentional); audit comment at L440 is acceptable historical breadcrumb; INDEX/README registration is complete; private-name cross-module import is not a current close blocker.

findings.issue: scripts/resolve-close-report.sh file mode 644 (not executable); peer convention is 755
findings.rationale: Verified via ls -la — single 644 outlier among ~40 peer 755 scripts. Round-2 NEW finding. Logged under non_functional_files[] with introduced_in_diff:true, blocks_close:false.

findings.issue: scripts/resolve-close-report.sh uses `set -u` only; peer scripts use `set -e`, `set -euo pipefail`, or `set -uo pipefail`
findings.rationale: Verified via grep ^set across scripts/*.sh — single outlier from established convention. Round-2 NEW finding. Logged under shell_conventions[] with introduced_in_diff:true, blocks_close:false. No runtime defect (explicit exits + no pipelines), but convention drift is real.

findings.issue: scripts/resolve-close-report.sh comment claims 'subproject path-walk' but implementation is a hard-coded 3-candidate probe; when CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR is unset, the first candidate becomes /docs/dev/... (FS-root anchored), non-empty but bogus
findings.proposed_fix: Either (a) reword the leading comment to drop 'path-walk' phrasing and describe the actual 3-candidate ordered probe, or (b) guard the project candidate with `[ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]` before pushing it into the loop
findings.rationale: Verified via shell experiment: `CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR="" bash -c 'echo "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}/docs/dev/X.md"'` prints `/docs/dev/X.md`. However, downstream `[ -f $candidate ]` correctly rejects the bogus path so no runtime defect — loop continues to next candidate, script's outcome unchanged. Round-2 NEW finding. Logged under historical_docs[] as comment-vs-code drift, minor, blocks_close:false. Codex's only substantive new concern in round 2.
Codex OBSERVATION_ONLY — `|| true` is intentional because the helper exits 1 (when no candidate exists) while still emitting the fallback path for the downstream file-exists check. No fix required.

findings.rationale: Re-confirmed in round 2. Logged under findings.imports[].

findings.rationale: Re-confirmed in round 2. Logged under findings.historical_docs[].

findings.rationale: Re-confirmed in round 2. Logged under findings.historical_docs[].
